name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-12
title: Add bypassPermissions to settings.json; guide users to put skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt in settings.local.json

history.note: Reopening: as-implemented split is wrong. Item shipped 'bypassPermissions in shared settings.json + warning-suppression in settings.local.json' per the item's literal text, but the underlying reasoning ('different users may want different behavior') applies equally to bypass mode itself — forcing all users into bypass mode via shared settings.json contradicts the per-user principle. Correct intent: BOTH bypassPermissions and skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt go in settings.local.json. /rabbit-config must manage the bypassPermissions key in settings.local.json (new subcommand). Behavior change for existing users — needs a session-init transition hint ('rabbit no longer forces bypass mode; run /rabbit-config bypass-permissions true if you want it').
